_doc: Per-team scheme-evolution landmarks. Each entry annotates a season with a coaching change, OC change, key acquisition, or scheme inflection. Edit by hand; reload picks up automatically. Start with DET, expand to other teams as the curation cycle warrants.

_schema.season: int — the season the landmark APPLIES TO (i.e., first season under the new HC/OC, year of the draft, etc.)
_schema.label: string — short headline shown on the chart
_schema.type: hc | oc | dc | qb | acquisition | scheme_inflection
_schema.story: string — 1-3 sentence context shown on hover/expand

DET.label: Cooter offense
DET.story: Jim Bob Cooter's last full year as OC under Caldwell. Stafford-led pass-first offense; explosive-pass attempts at career highs.

DET.label: Patricia HC
DET.story: Matt Patricia hired as HC after Caldwell era. Defensive-minded coach; offensive identity drifts. Roster turnover begins.

DET.label: Bevell OC
DET.story: Darrell Bevell takes over as OC. More play-action, deeper drop-back game; the original Russell-Wilson-era Seattle template ported to Detroit.

DET.label: Campbell HC + Lynn OC
DET.story: Dan Campbell hired as HC; Anthony Lynn hired as OC. Reset season — Goff acquired in Stafford trade. Run-heavy, conservative passing identity for one year.

DET.label: Ben Johnson OC ⚡
DET.story: Ben Johnson promoted from passing-game coordinator to OC. Offense transforms — pre-snap motion, condensed sets, aggressive intermediate passing. Lions become a top-5 offense by EPA.

DET.label: Gibbs + LaPorta drafted
DET.story: Jahmyr Gibbs (1st round) + Sam LaPorta (2nd round) join. RB-receiving and TE usage explode. Passing distribution shifts toward intermediate/short.

DET.label: Peak Ben Johnson
DET.story: 15-2 regular-season run. Lions offense leads the NFL in EPA per play. Ben Johnson interviews for HC jobs. Most likely his last year as Lions OC.

DET.label: post-Ben Johnson era
DET.story: OC transition. Watch the route distribution: any shift away from the Ben Johnson template signals a real philosophical change vs continuity hire.

BAL.label: Lamar takes over
BAL.story: Lamar Jackson replaces Joe Flacco mid-season. Run-heavy designed-QB scheme begins. Greg Roman builds offense around Lamar's mobility.

BAL.label: Todd Monken OC
BAL.story: Greg Roman out, Todd Monken in. Offense modernizes — more play-action, more intermediate passing. Lamar wins MVP. Receiver-route distribution evolves materially.

BAL.label: Derrick Henry signs
BAL.story: Henry's arrival shifts run/pass split and play-action efficiency. Mark Andrews + Hopkins absorb intermediate passing share.

KC.label: Mahomes era begins
KC.story: Patrick Mahomes takes over as starter. Reid offense unleashed — deep ball + Tyreek Hill + Travis Kelce becomes the league's most dangerous trio.

KC.label: Tyreek-less offense
KC.story: First full year without Tyreek Hill (traded 2022). Kelce + Rice become route-tree centerpieces. Distribution shifts shorter / more underneath.
